 {"id":"bd-8sho","title":"bd-1j4 subtask: Hard-parts docs/parity/support-matrix truth reconciliation","description":"Background:
- Hard-parts completion claims require synchronized docs/support matrix/feature parity narratives.

Goal:
- Reconcile hard-parts implementation reality with user-facing claims and machine-generated reports.

Deliverables:
1) Hard-parts status truth table across README/FEATURE_PARITY/support_matrix/reality report.
2) Drift guard updates for hard-parts claim consistency.
3) Explicit “deferred vs implemented” rationale for each hard-parts subsystem.

Acceptance Criteria:
- No contradictory hard-parts claims across docs/artifacts.
- Drift gates catch future inconsistency automatically.

Verification & Logging:
- Drift guard tests and fixtures.
- Structured logs for reconciliation checks and mismatches.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RedMaple","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:33.576518493Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T08:52:20.345066285Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T08:52:20.345031921Z","close_reason":"Completed hard-parts truth table reconciliation across README/FEATURE_PARITY/support_matrix/reality_report with new drift guard script, harness test, and CI wiring.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","docs","drift","hard-parts","verification"],"comments":[{"id":1063,"issue_id":"bd-8sho","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"RedMaple claiming bd-8sho and starting hard-parts truth reconciliation across README/FEATURE_PARITY/support_matrix/reality_report.\n\nPlanned deliverables:\n1)  artifact with per-subsystem implemented vs deferred rationale.\n2)  drift guard script.\n3)  guard test.\n4) doc updates in  and  to include explicit hard-parts truth lines tied to the artifact.","created_at":"2026-02-13T08:48:02Z"},{"id":1064,"issue_id":"bd-8sho","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update (RedMaple): completed hard-parts docs/parity/support/reality truth reconciliation with drift guard wiring.\n\nImplemented:\n- New canonical artifact: tests/conformance/hard_parts_truth_table.v1.json\n  - per-subsystem status + implemented/deferred scope rationale for startup/threading/resolver/nss/locale/iconv\n  - support_matrix expectations per subsystem\n  - reality_report snapshot binding + contradiction summary\n- New drift guard: scripts/check_hard_parts_truth.sh\n  - validates artifact schema, README + FEATURE_PARITY hard-parts truth lines, support_matrix expectations, and reality_report snapshot alignment\n  - emits structured JSON event with trace_id/mode/api_family/outcome/artifact refs\n- New harness test: crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/hard_parts_truth_test.rs\n  - artifact schema validation\n  - guard script execution gate\n- Docs reconciled:\n  - README.md hard-parts truth section added\n  - FEATURE_PARITY.md hard-parts truth section added\n- CI wiring:\n  - scripts/ci.sh now runs scripts/check_hard_parts_truth.sh\n\nVerification executed (PASS):\n- scripts/check_hard_parts_truth.sh\n- CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/cargo-target-codex-harness cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test hard_parts_truth_test\n- CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/cargo-target-codex-harness cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test parity_report_drift_test\n\nNote:\n- /tmp is full on this host; verification used CARGO_TARGET_DIR under /data/tmp for deterministic local runs.","created_at":"2026-02-13T08:52:17Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-8zsp","title":"Smoke-fuzz proptests for inet IP parsers","description":"parse_ipv4, parse_ipv6, inet_pton, inet_aton in frankenlibc_core::inet are classic security-sensitive parsers. Add Archetype-1 smoke-fuzz proptests: (a) never-panics on arbitrary bytes, (b) output bounded, (c) format-roundtrip for inputs that parse successfully (parse(format(x)) == x). Scope: crates/frankenlibc-core/src/inet/mod.rs #[cfg(test)] module.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T20:29:16.512627925Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T20:31:46.178725431Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T20:31:46.176686404Z","close_reason":"Added 8 inet smoke-fuzz proptests in commit 0cf34de: parse_ipv4 (never-panics, structured-alphabet, round-trip), parse_ipv6 (never-panics, structured-alphabet, round-trip), inet_pton (af-cube), inet_aton (legacy). ~4100 case evaluations per run, no panics found on first sweep. Covers historical CVE surface for dotted-quad arithmetic and IPv6 '::' expansion.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-95tq","title":"resolv: gai_cancel and gai_suspend empty requests should return EAI_ALLDONE","description":"Concrete regression: host glibc returns EAI_ALLDONE with errno unchanged for synchronous-degenerate async DNS calls like gai_cancel(NULL), gai_cancel(&zeroed_gaicb), gai_suspend(NULL, 0, timeout), and gai_suspend([NULL], 1, timeout). FrankenLibC currently returns EAI_SYSTEM with ENOSYS. Restore host-parity degenerate semantics in unistd_abi.rs and lock them with focused ABI tests.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T06:14:54.765525235Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T06:19:52.577129952Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T06:19:52.574907623Z","close_reason":"Restored host-parity EAI_ALLDONE semantics for synchronous-degenerate gai_cancel/gai_suspend inputs and verified locally plus via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
@@ -580,6 +582,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-clua","title":"resolv: add host-parity ABI probes for synchronous-only async-DNS wrappers","description":"Capture concrete glibc behavior for getaddrinfo_a/gai_error/gai_cancel/gai_suspend degenerate cases in focused ABI probes so future resolver stub work is grounded in observed host semantics instead of assumptions.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T06:27:54.963463136Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T06:27:54.963463136Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-cmiv","title":"EPIC: Implement real DNS resolution for getaddrinfo","description":"getaddrinfo() currently only resolves:
1. Numeric IP addresses (127.0.0.1, ::1)
2. Hostnames found in /etc/hosts

It does NOT perform actual DNS queries. This means programs like wget, curl (for non-localhost URLs), dig, nslookup, and any network client that resolves hostnames will fail.

OPTIONS:
A) Delegate to host libc's getaddrinfo for non-numeric, non-/etc/hosts hostnames
B) Implement a minimal DNS stub resolver (send UDP query to nameserver from /etc/resolv.conf)
C) Both: try our resolver first, fall back to host

Option A is fastest to implement and most reliable. Option B gives us full independence from host libc.

ALREADY DONE: /etc/hosts lookup works, numeric IP parsing works, freeaddrinfo correctly frees our Box-allocated structures.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:15.603558212Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T18:43:31.403160236Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T18:43:31.402959135Z","close_reason":"Already implemented. getaddrinfo has a full native DNS stub resolver: udp_dns_query() sends A/AAAA queries to /etc/resolv.conf nameservers via SYS_socket/SYS_sendto/SYS_recvfrom with configurable timeout/retry. ResolverConfig parses nameservers, search domains, ndots. Resolution chain: numeric -> /etc/hosts -> DNS -> repair fallback. 46 resolv tests pass. Previously verified when closing bd-zh1y.7 and bd-1y7.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-cmiv.1","title":"Add host libc delegation for DNS-requiring getaddrinfo calls","description":"When getaddrinfo receives a hostname that:
1. Is NOT a numeric IP address
2. Is NOT found in /etc/hosts

Delegate to the host libc's getaddrinfo via our ELF resolver. The host's getaddrinfo will perform the actual DNS query.

IMPLEMENTATION:
1. resolve_host_symbol_raw("getaddrinfo") to get host's function pointer
2. Call host_getaddrinfo(node, service, hints, res) 
3. Return the host's result directly (the addrinfo structs are heap-allocated by the host)
4. In freeaddrinfo, detect whether the addrinfo was allocated by us (Box) or host (malloc) and free accordingly

TRICKY PART: freeaddrinfo must distinguish our Box-allocated addrinfos from host-malloc-allocated ones. Could use a sentinel field or check if the pointer is in our Box allocation range.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:15.811880807Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:30:57.450087855Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:30:57.448613314Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-cmiv.1","depends_on_id":"bd-cmiv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:15.811880807Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Add ABI regression coverage for that precedence.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:39:25.750825423Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T08:39:25.750825423Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-cs9w","title":"Left-Right Sync for complex metadata reads (section 14.3)","description":"Implement Left-Right synchronization for wait-free reads of complex metadata structures in FrankenLibC. Left-Right maintains two copies of the data structure (left and right). Readers always access one copy wait-free (no locks, no atomics on the read path). Writers update the inactive copy, then toggle the active pointer, then wait for readers to drain from the old copy before updating it too. Compared to RCU (bd-3aof), Left-Right provides: (a) Wait-free reads (not just lock-free). (b) Works for non-RCU-friendly data structures (those requiring consistent snapshots of multiple fields). Application to FrankenLibC: (1) Allocator configuration — the allocator's configuration (size-class table, arena count, thread-to-arena mapping) is a complex multi-field structure that must be read consistently. Left-Right provides atomic snapshot reads without locks. (2) TSM policy table — the TSM's decision policy (state machine transitions, threshold parameters) may be updated at runtime (e.g., when switching from strict to hardened mode). Left-Right ensures readers always see a consistent policy snapshot. (3) Symbol dispatch table — the IFUNC-like dispatch table mapping symbols to implementations. Updated when CPU features change (e.g., AVX-512 becomes available after cpuid check). Implementation: LeftRight<T> wrapper with read() -> &T (wait-free) and write(|t: &mut T| ...) (serialized, may block briefly).

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","design":"**Alien CS Reference:** Section 14.3 of graveyard. Left-Right from Ramalhete & Correia (2015). Wait-free reads with consistent snapshots for multi-field structures.

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- LeftRight<T: Clone>: maintains two copies (left: Box<T>, right: Box<T>) with version_index: AtomicBool.
- read() -> ReadGuard<T>: returns reference to active copy. No atomic RMW on read path (single atomic load of version_index).
- ReadGuard registers in per-reader arrival/departure counters (2 counters, one per version).
- write(|t: &mut T| ...) -> (): acquires writer mutex, updates inactive copy, toggles version_index, waits for readers on old version to drain, updates other copy.
- Reader drain: spin on reader counter for old version until zero. Bounded wait if readers are well-behaved.
- T: Clone required to synchronize the two copies after toggle.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
1. LeftRight<T> compiles and passes miri for basic read/write cycle.
2. Read path is wait-free: no CAS, no RMW (verified by cargo asm inspection).
3. Consistent snapshot: multi-field read always sees consistent state (test with 2-field struct updated atomically).
4. 16-reader + 1-writer stress test: 1M operations, TSan clean.
5. Read latency <3ns at p99 (compare with RCU at <2ns and RwLock at ~15ns).
6. Writer latency <1us including reader drain wait at p99 under 16-reader load.
7. No starvation: writer completes within bounded time even with continuous readers.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"**Logging Requirements:**
- tracing::trace!(target: left_right, operation=read, version, reader_count) per read (disabled in release).
- tracing::debug!(target: left_right, operation=write, version_toggled, drain_wait_ns) per write.
- tracing::warn!(target: left_right, slow_drain, drain_wait_ns, remaining_readers) if drain exceeds 100us.

### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:29:32.674331698Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T18:34:45.507096513Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T18:34:45.506818727Z","close_reason":"Implemented LeftRight synchronization in frankenlibc-membrane with cached readers, drain coordination, diagnostics, and 11 focused tests. Verified with rch exec cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane left_right -- --nocapture, rch exec cargo check --workspace --all-targets, and rch exec cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-membrane --all-targets -- -D warnings. Full-workspace clippy/fmt remain blocked by unrelated pre-existing ABI-file issues in the dirty worktree.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-cs9w","depends_on_id":"bd-3aof","type":"related","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-d5l","title":"Perf: Microbench RuntimeMathKernel::decide/observe (criterion + ns/op)","description":"Create a repeatable benchmark that measures runtime_math overhead directly.

Acceptance criteria:
- Bench measures decide() and observe_validation_result() separately and together.
- Reports p50/p95/p99 ns/op and throughput.
- Produces stable output suitable for regression gating.

Implementation notes:
- Keep benchmark deterministic: fixed inputs, fixed warmup, pin thread if needed.
- Include both strict and hardened mode paths.
- Record compiler flags / CPU model in output metadata.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BlueLake","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:30:56.703138634Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T02:10:24.254231420Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T02:10:24.254213887Z","close_reason":"bench implemented","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":1081,"issue_id":"bd-d5l","author":"BlueLake","text":"Implemented criterion microbench  (decide / observe_validation_result / decide+observe) in  and wired it in .

How to run:
- Strict: RUNTIME_MATH_BENCH_META glibc_rust_mode_env=strict
RUNTIME_MATH_BENCH_META rustflags=<unset>
RUNTIME_MATH_BENCH_META cpu_model=: AMD Ryzen Threadripper PRO 5995WX 64-Cores
runtime_math/decide/strict
                        time:   [1.0968 µs 1.1060 µs 1.1172 µs]
                        thrpt:  [895.13 Kelem/s 904.19 Kelem/s 911.75 Kelem/s]
                 change:
                        time:   [-1.9912% +0.0240% +2.0613%] (p = 0.99 > 0.05)
                        thrpt:  [-2.0197% -0.0240% +2.0316%]
                        No change in performance detected.
Found 16 outliers among 100 measurements (16.00%)
  2 (2.00%) high mild
  14 (14.00%) high severe

RUNTIME_MATH_BENCH mode=strict bench=decide samples=110 p50_ns_op=1093.831 p95_ns_op=1637.438 p99_ns_op=1727.887 mean_ns_op=1186.927 throughput_ops_s=901560.862
runtime_math/observe_fast/strict
                        time:   [3.4119 µs 3.4687 µs 3.5429 µs]
                        thrpt:  [282.25 Kelem/s 288.29 Kelem/s 293.09 Kelem/s]
                 change:
                        time:   [-5.3607% -0.0676% +5.4778%] (p = 0.98 > 0.05)
                        thrpt:  [-5.1934% +0.0677% +5.6644%]
                        No change in performance detected.

What changed:
- Added crates/glibc-rs-bench/benches/runtime_math_bench.rs
- Added [[bench]] runtime_math_bench entry in crates/glibc-rs-bench/Cargo.toml

Bench targets:
- runtime_math/decide/<mode>
- runtime_math/observe_fast/<mode>
- runtime_math/decide_observe/<mode>

How to run:
- Strict: GLIBC_RUST_MODE=strict cargo bench -p glibc-rs-bench --bench runtime_math_bench
- Hardened: GLIBC_RUST_MODE=hardened cargo bench -p glibc-rs-bench --bench runtime_math_bench

Output:
- RUNTIME_MATH_BENCH_META lines (env mode, RUSTFLAGS, CPU model)
- RUNTIME_MATH_BENCH lines with p50/p95/p99/mean ns/op and throughput ops/s

Determinism:
- fixed inputs (pointer_validation ctx)
- fixed warmup loops (10k iters)
- optional Linux CPU pinning: GLIBC_RUST_BENCH_PIN=1","created_at":"2026-02-10T02:10:05Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-dfe","title":"EPIC: Inventory + drift control (AGENTS.md vs runtime_math code; snapshot schema stability)","description":"Goal: prevent architectural drift.

Why:
- runtime_math has many moving parts; if AGENTS.md lists required modules/decision law but code diverges, future work becomes inconsistent.

Outputs:
- A repeatable check that module inventory + decision law in AGENTS.md matches code.
- A stable, versioned RuntimeKernelSnapshot schema (new fields are additive and documented).
- Fusion signal vector size always matches number of cached states.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:30:15.669320398Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T02:13:43.636260651Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T02:13:36.812282675Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-dfe","depends_on_id":"bd-1az","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-dfe","depends_on_id":"bd-1tx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-dfe","depends_on_id":"bd-1wy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-dfe","depends_on_id":"bd-29r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-dfe","depends_on_id":"bd-31i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-dfe","depends_on_id":"bd-ju7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-dfe","depends_on_id":"bd-p5i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1083,"issue_id":"bd-dfe","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Epic Notes: Inventory + Drift Control

### Why We Need This
This repo is intentionally ambitious: lots of runtime_math modules and many moving parts. Drift is the default failure mode.

We must continuously ensure:
- AGENTS.md module inventory matches code reality
- decide() implementation matches the documented decision law
- snapshot schemas are stable and versioned
- fused signals are consistent across modules

### What This Epic Produces
- automated checks (compile-time + tests) that fail loudly on drift
- a wiring checklist so adding kernels doesn’t silently omit snapshots/tests
- conservative merge rules for decision-law changes (behavior proofs required)

### Definition Of Done
- We can answer: “why did runtime choose FullValidate here?” with a stable policy_id + evidence.
- A new runtime_math module can’t land without:
  - being registered
  - being snapshotted
  - being represented in fusion signals (if relevant)
  - being covered by at least one test","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:52:17Z"},{"id":1084,"issue_id":"bd-dfe","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CobaltCompass: All 7 blockers CLOSED. check_module_inventory.sh (68/68 in sync), check_module_wiring.sh, check_snapshot_coverage.sh (68/68 covered), snapshot_gate.sh, perf_gate.sh all passing. Schema versioning, cadence classification, fusion consistency, decision-law validation all delivered.","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:13:43Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-s39o.3","title":"Test and fix network diagnostic tools under LD_PRELOAD (wget, dig, nslookup)","description":"Test wget, dig, nslookup, host under LD_PRELOAD.
These exercise: DNS resolution (getaddrinfo beyond /etc/hosts), socket connect, SSL/TLS, HTTP parsing.
Our getaddrinfo only supports numeric hosts and /etc/hosts lookups. For DNS resolution, we'd need to either:
1. Delegate to host libc's getaddrinfo for non-numeric hosts
2. Implement a DNS resolver stub
Option 1 is more practical for now.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:54.277849234Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T05:13:31.400999331Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T05:13:30.152849132Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-s39o.3","depends_on_id":"bd-cmiv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:16.988805266Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-s39o.3","depends_on_id":"bd-s39o","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:54.277849234Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1338,"issue_id":"bd-s39o.3","author":"ubuntu","text":"wget resolves DNS, connects, but GnuTLS has thread locking error (separate SSL issue). curl works perfectly including HTTPS. dig/nslookup are statically linked (can't preload). host works.","created_at":"2026-03-25T05:13:31Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-z7gt","title":"bd-3bg subtask: Iconv conformance+reproducibility gates with structured evidence","description":"Background:
- iconv completion claims require end-to-end evidence, not just generator/runtime unit tests.

Goal:
- Deliver conformance + reproducibility verification pack for iconv pipeline and runtime behavior.

Deliverables:
1) Conversion fixtures for included codecs.
2) Reproducibility gate artifacts for generated tables.
3) Drift checks tying docs/support matrix to implementation reality.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Conversion fixtures and reproducibility gates pass in CI.
- Evidence artifacts are complete and reviewable.

Verification & Logging:
- E2E fixture scripts with strict/hardened runs where relevant.
- Structured logs: trace_id, fixture_id, codec_pair, expected, actual, timing, artifact_refs.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria

### Mandatory Verification Gate
1. Unit Test Evidence
- Add deterministic unit tests for nominal, boundary, and invalid-input paths in the touched modules.
- Include strict and hardened mode assertions when behavior differs by runtime mode.
- Include failure-mode tests covering repair/deny/default behavior with explicit expected outputs.

2. Integration + E2E Evidence
- Add or extend fixture-driven integration coverage in `tests/conformance` and/or `tests/integration` for this bead scope.
- Add a deterministic E2E script path (or harness command) with explicit setup/teardown and replayable seeds.
- Compare FrankenLibC outputs against host glibc fixtures (or documented replacement-level contract) for all touched symbols.

3. Logging + Traceability Evidence
- Emit structured logs (JSONL or deterministic text) that include bead ID, scenario ID, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, and outcome.
- Verify logs expose repair/deny counters and reason codes so regressions are diagnosable from artifacts alone.
- Record artifact paths in bead notes (test logs, fixture diffs, benchmark outputs, conformance reports).

4. Performance + Regression Evidence
- Capture before/after baseline for latency/throughput on touched hot paths.
- Enforce no-regression threshold or document bounded tradeoff with quantitative rationale.

5. CI Gate Evidence
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo check --all-targets`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --all-targets`
- all bead-specific harness and E2E scripts pass with deterministic logs archived.","notes":"## Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- [ ] Unit tests added/updated for happy-path, edge-case, and invalid-input behavior.
- [ ] Property-style or fuzz-seed replay coverage added where state space is large/combinatorial.
- [ ] Integration fixtures added/updated in `tests/conformance` and validated against host glibc expectations.
- [ ] Deterministic E2E script/runner added or updated with explicit command lines and seed controls.
- [ ] Strict vs hardened behavior assertions are explicit and versioned in fixtures.
- [ ] Structured logs include: timestamp, bead ID, test scenario, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, action (allow/repair/deny), and result.
- [ ] Failure logs include reason codes + minimal reproducible input payload metadata.
- [ ] Benchmark/perf logs include baseline, candidate, delta %, and pass/fail gate decision.
- [ ] CI artifacts include conformance report, E2E transcript, and summarized pass/fail matrix.
- [ ] Release/merge gate blocked if any required test or log artifact is missing.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Codex","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:01:54.584467417Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-13T23:56:39.065467719Z","closed_at":"2026-04-13T23:56:39.065262901Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","e2e","iconv","logging","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-z7gt","depends_on_id":"bd-13ya","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-z7gt","depends_on_id":"bd-by8c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-z84","title":"Futex mutex core: implement pthread_mutex_* state machine without glibc call-through","description":"Critique mapping: #2.

Deliverables:
- Futex-backed mutex lock/unlock/trylock implementation for NORMAL mutexes first.
- Deterministic contention path and fairness notes.
- Evidence counters for spin/futex-wait/futex-wake branches.

Acceptance:
- pthread mutex fixtures pass under strict and hardened modes.
- No libc::pthread_mutex_* symbol call-through in replacement profile.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","notes":"2026-02-11 progress (WhiteMeadow): Reworked pthread mutex ABI path to futex-backed managed mutexes with foreign/static fallback routing. Added managed marker (MANAGED_MUTEX_MAGIC) and host fallback wrappers via glibc __pthread_mutex_* symbols. Verified no direct libc::pthread_mutex_(init|destroy|lock|trylock|unlock) calls remain in crates/glibc-rs-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs. Validation: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/wm-target cargo check -p glibc-rs-abi (pass); scripts/check_replacement_guard.sh interpose (pass, total call-through reduced to 71). Blocking issue: LD_PRELOAD runtime still hangs for mutex fixture runs (timeout in strict+hardened), including /tmp/fixture_mutex_only and c_fixture_suite results at target/c_fixture_suite/20260211T075055Z/results.json; this appears broader startup/interposition behavior beyond direct pthread_mutex_* call-through removal. Bead remains in_progress pending strict/hardened fixture pass criteria.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SnowyCat","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:09.289486815Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-19T19:21:31.436392216Z","closed_at":"2026-02-19T19:21:30.940330933Z","close_reason":"Acceptance criteria met: strict+hardened mutex fixture pass + zero forbidden pthread_mutex call-through","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","futex","pthread"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-z84","depends_on_id":"bd-19j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-z84","depends_on_id":"bd-1qy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-z84","depends_on_id":"bd-1uc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-z84","depends_on_id":"bd-300","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-z84","depends_on_id":"bd-327","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1234,"issue_id":"bd-z84","author":"Codex","text":"Codex here. This clears the bd-1qy dependency blocker from a fixture/logging perspective.","created_at":"2026-02-13T06:43:52Z"},{"id":1236,"issue_id":"bd-z84","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Validated bd-z84 acceptance with fresh evidence on 2026-02-19:

1) pthread mutex fixture pass in strict+hardened under preload
- Command: `FIXTURE_FILTER=fixture_pthread_mutex_adversarial.c TIMEOUT_SECONDS=8 BEAD_ID=bd-z84 rch exec -- bash scripts/c_fixture_suite.sh`

- Artifacts:
